prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.788) and very high seasonality variance (1.0907), with ETS capturing seasonality best (0.8534) while DWT_rf tracks trends most accurately (0.8717). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, down, down), indicating non-stationary patterns that require flexible combination methods. Early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, THETA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, THETA), suggesting per-horizon selection could help, but the high disagreement and seasonality variance strongly favor DBA combination which can handle both trend and seasonal pattern disagreements effectively.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_rf", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.788,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.788) and very high seasonality variance (1.0907), with ETS capturing seasonality best (0.8534) while DWT_rf tracks trends most accurately (0.8717). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, down, down), indicating non-stationary patterns that require flexible combination methods. Early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, THETA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, THETA), suggesting per-horizon selection could help, but the high disagreement and seasonality variance strongly favor DBA combination which can handle both trend and seasonal pattern disagreements effectively."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.275 ≥ 0.25 signals high disagreement favoring robust methods like dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). RMSE spread between THETA (2440.91) and CWT_svr (4269.30) is ~74%, justifying shrinkage=0.35 in inverse_rmse_weights. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling both trend_champion (DWT_rf) and seasonality_champion (ETS) disagreements."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": ["best_single_by_validation"],
  "remove_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has good diversity (2 selection, 1 baseline, 1 weighted). However, universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation significantly outperforms all current candidates: RMSE=2438.8 vs dba_combination's 2562.7, SMAPE=0.267 vs topk_mean_per_horizon_k5's 0.281, MAPE=0.312 vs topk_mean_per_horizon_k5's 0.334. Adding this high-performing selection candidate and removing weakest by score (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at 0.814) improves performance while maintaining diversity. All current candidates are leakage-safe: dba_combination is baseline (never leaks), others use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3.",
  "changes": ["Added best_single_by_validation from universe RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE leaderboards", "Removed topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 (lowest score 0.814)"],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.788) and very high seasonality variance (1.0907), with ETS capturing seasonality best (0.8534) while DWT_rf tracks trends most accurately (0.8717). The true series shows mixed trend directions across folds (up, down, down), indicating non-stationary patterns that require flexible combination methods. Early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, THETA) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, THETA), suggesting per-horizon selection could help, but the high disagreement and seasonality variance strongly favor DBA combination which can handle both trend and seasonal pattern disagreements effectively.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.275 ≥ 0.25 signals high disagreement favoring robust methods like dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). RMSE spread between THETA (2440.91) and CWT_svr (4269.30) is ~74%, justifying shrinkage=0.35 in inverse_rmse_weights. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling both trend_champion (DWT_rf) and seasonality_champion (ETS) disagreements.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has good diversity (2 selection, 1 baseline, 1 weighted). However, universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation significantly outperforms all current candidates: RMSE=2438.8 vs dba_combination's 2562.7, SMAPE=0.267 vs topk_mean_per_horizon_k5's 0.281, MAPE=0.312 vs topk_mean_per_horizon_k5's 0.334. Adding this high-performing selection candidate and removing weakest by score (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at 0.814) improves performance while maintaining diversity. All current candidates are leakage-safe: dba_combination is baseline (never leaks), others use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3.
changes: Added best_single_by_validation from universe RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE leaderboards
